title: parietaria officinalis var. ramiflora
intro: Parietaria officinalis var. ramiflora, commonly known as the woodland or creeping Pellitory, is a flowering plant species in the Nettle family, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. Traditionally, Parietaria officinalis var. ramiflora has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to have a positive effect on various health conditions. The medicinal properties of this plant are said to provide relief from headaches, fever, and skin irritations, as well as offering potential benefits for digestive issues and respiratory problems. Parietaria officinalis var. ramiflora can be consumed in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
